evals.name: python-api-no-plan
evals.prompt: Review this Python API code for bugs. The diff is at skills/code-review/evals/files/eval1.diff and the full source is at skills/code-review/evals/files/eval1_api.py.
evals.expected_output: A REVIEW.md identifying mutable default argument in validate_email, bare except in delete_user, thread-safety issues on _users dict (lock imported but never used), naive datetime.now() calls, and get_user returning None without caller warning. Verdict should be REQUEST CHANGES due to critical defects.
expectations: Review identifies mutable default argument bug in validate_email (allowed_domains=[])
Review identifies bare except clause in delete_user as overly broad (catches BaseException including KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit)
Review identifies thread-safety issue: _lock is imported but never used, while _users dict is mutated from multiple functions without synchronization
Review identifies datetime.now() without timezone producing naive datetimes
Review identifies get_user returning None silently when caller may expect a dict
Every defect finding includes a file:line reference
Every defect finding includes a confidence tag ([confirmed], [likely], or [possible])
Every defect explains why it is a problem, not just what it is
Review includes a Change Summary section inferring the intent of the code
Review includes a Verdict (REQUEST CHANGES, APPROVE WITH SUGGESTIONS, or APPROVE)
Empty severity sections say 'None identified.' rather than being omitted
anti_expectations: Does not flag style issues, formatting, naming conventions, or missing docstrings
Does not produce false positives on correct code patterns
Does not suggest running tests or linting
Does not include [possible] confidence findings unless in high blast radius areas or defect section would otherwise be empty

evals.name: typescript-with-plan
evals.prompt: Review this TypeScript task manager against its plan. Plan is at skills/code-review/evals/files/eval2_plan.md, diff at skills/code-review/evals/files/eval2.diff, and full source at skills/code-review/evals/files/eval2_task_manager.ts.
evals.expected_output: A REVIEW.md with plan compliance section showing: runTask fails to await handler (acceptance criterion #2 violated), runAllPending uses forEach with async instead of Promise.all (criterion #3), parseTaskConfig has no input validation (criterion #4), == used instead of === (criterion #5), findTask return type mismatch (criterion #6). Also flags prototype pollution in mergeTaskMetadata via Object.assign with user input. Verdict should be REQUEST CHANGES.
expectations: Review includes a Plan Summary section summarizing the plan's intent
Review includes a Plan Compliance section with Implemented, Missing or Incomplete, and Deviations subsections
Review identifies missing await on handler() call in runTask (async function result assigned without await)
Review identifies forEach with async callback in runAllPending does not actually await tasks — plan requires Promise.all or equivalent
Review identifies JSON.parse on untrusted input without validation in parseTaskConfig (plan criterion #4)
Review identifies == used instead of === for status comparisons in runAllPending and deleteCompletedTasks (plan criterion #5)
Review identifies findTask return type mismatch: function declares return type Task but can return undefined (plan criterion #6)
Review identifies prototype pollution risk in mergeTaskMetadata via Object.assign with user-controlled input
Every defect finding includes a file:line reference and confidence tag
Verdict is REQUEST CHANGES due to critical plan compliance failures
anti_expectations: Does not flag style issues, formatting, or naming conventions
Does not critique the plan's architecture — only checks code against the plan

evals.name: empty-diff-stops-early
evals.prompt: Review my latest changes. I've been working on the auth module.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects an empty diff (no staged or unstaged changes in working tree) and informs the user there are no changes to review. Does NOT write a REVIEW.md file.
expectations: Collects the diff using git diff HEAD (or equivalent) to check for changes
Detects that the diff is empty (no changes to review)
Informs the user there are no changes to review
Does NOT write a REVIEW.md file
Does NOT fabricate findings or produce a review of non-existent changes
anti_expectations: Does not write a REVIEW.md file when there is no diff
Does not hallucinate code changes or findings
Does not proceed with the full review workflow when the diff is empty
